{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middletier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|7th century CE burial ground in Japan}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kurumazuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|13.9|N|139|48|34.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mibu, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''|車塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/474 |title=車塚古墳|trans-title= Kurumazuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurumazuka Kofun is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''[[kofun]]'' located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town. It is one of the largest round Kofuns in Japan and is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Ushizuka Kofun is adjacent to the western side, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 500 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has a diameter of 82 meters and a height of eight meters, with a flat surface on the top of the dome. It was constructed in three tiers, with the diameter of 82 meters at the bottom, 52 meters at the middletier and 32 meters at the upper tier. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', some traces of which remain, but no ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed that it was once surrounded by an outer dike and two rings of moats with a width of 7.5 meters. The tomb was pillaged in ancient times, leaving only the well-preserved stone horizontal [[passage grave]]-style [[burial chamber]] made from blocks of [[tuff]], which has been open to the elements since at least the [[Edo period]]. The burial chamber has dimensions of three meters long by 2.8 meters wide by 2.3 meters high with a 1.35 meter high entrance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The lack of ''haniwa'' or [[grave goods]] complicates dating, but the tumulus is estimated to date from the early 7th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] and the [[Asuka period]] when the [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings and the centralized [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kurumazuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[Mibu Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
